results.sample_name: High Sensitivity - Medical with Small Community
results.original_text: My name is Sarah Chen and I live in Millbrook, a small town in Dutchess County, NY (population ~1,500). I'm 34 years old and was diagnosed with Ehlers-Danlos syndrome hypermobility type last March at the Mayo Clinic. I've been experiencing severe joint dislocations, chronic fatigue, and recently developed POTS. My cardiologist Dr. Williams started me on midodrine and compression therapy. I'm also dealing with anxiety because my employer at the local elementary school doesn't understand why I need so many sick days. Last month I fainted during a school assembly and it was really embarrassing—the whole town is talking about it now.

results.original_text: I've been having trouble at work lately. My manager keeps scheduling me for early morning shifts even though I told HR about my sleep disorder. I take medication that makes it hard to wake up early. Last week I was late three times and got a written warning. I'm worried about losing my job but I don't want to disclose more details about my condition. I work at a small accounting firm downtown with only 12 employees.

results.original_text: I've been experiencing occasional headaches for the past few weeks, usually in the afternoon. They're not severe but they're annoying. I drink plenty of water and try to take breaks from my computer screen. Should I be concerned? What over-the-counter medications would you recommend for tension headaches?
